hypothesis: If thermodynamic data, cellular automata simulation, machine learning property prediction, and commercial database integration are combined into a unified computational workflow, then it will be possible to systematically identify cost-effective and thermodynamically favorable synthesis routes for target inorganic materials (such as Ca2SiO4) from a large chemical space of candidate reactants, because the multi-module approach simultaneously optimizes reaction probability, product feasibility, and economic constraints in a way that no single method could achieve alone.
